item.title: Business and Trade Committee publishes correspondence with Royal Mail, EP Group, CWU and Ofcom on Royal Mail’s performance
item.description: The Business and Trade Committee is today publishing correspondence with Royal Mail, EP Group, CWU and Ofcom on Royal Mail’s performance and improvement plans.

item.title: Committee launches inquiry into UK–US trade deal amid concerns it is not yet delivering growth
item.description: The Business and Trade Committee has today launched its new stocktake inquiry into the UK’s economic relationship with the United States

item.title: Committee launches stocktake of UK–EU economic relations as reset risks falling short
item.description: Ten years after the referendum, the UK’s economic relationship with the European Union remains decisive

item.title: Business and Trade Committee Chair responds to Ming Yang investment block
item.description: Rt Hon Liam Byrne MP, Chair of the Business and Trade Committee responds to reports that the Government has decided to block Chinese company Ming Yang’s plans to build a factory in Scotland on national security grounds.

item.title: New guarantees needed from ministers in G7 sovereign steel gamble
item.description: Rt Liam Byrne MP, Chair of the Committee, comment on the UK Government steel strategy

item.title: Post Office Horizon IT scandal: “serious structural failings persist” in redress
item.description: One year on from the Committee’s last report on the Post Office Horizon scandal, progress has been made delivering redress for victims of the Fujitsu-supplied Horizon IT scandal. More than 11,300 claimants have received payments with £1.44 billion distributed.

item.title: Royal Mail’s reliance on headline letter delivery figures can’t hide service failures as 219 million letters arrive late
item.description: Last month the Business and Trade Committee launched a snap investigation into Royal Mail’s letter delivery service, after widespread reports of late and missing letters, or letters bunched together for delivery days after they were due.

item.title: New BTC inquiry: China and the UK Economy - what is the prize, and what is the price?
item.description: Today, as China’s National People’s Congress opens in Beijing, the Business and Trade Committee launches a major new inquiry into China and the UK economy.

item.title: Committee concerns on role of CMA Chair require risk mitigations from ministers – Pre-Appointment Report
item.description: Committee concerns focus on significant potential conflicts of interest and on challenge to Ministers in investment decisions that deliver market consolidation over improved consumer welfare

item.title: Small businesses facing pressures comparable to the pandemic, Business and Trade Committee finds
item.description: “High streets don’t die by accident” – BTC sets out series of reforms to end late payments, overhaul business rates, cut costs and boost revenue for small businesses.

item.title: BTC: UK economic security regime is not fit for the future; Government’s response inadequate in face of the threat
item.description: Government rejects reforms as threats from hostile states mount

item.title: Business and Trade Committee Sets Seven Priorities for UK Economic Revival
item.description: Committee launches ambitious agenda targeting AI transformation, industrial strategy, and economic security.

item.title: UK – India Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement: Committee warns Government staff cuts put £billions in tariff savings at risk
item.description: The Business and Trade Committee today warns that billions of pounds of tariffs savings from the UK’s trade deal with India could be jeopardised by deep cuts to UK export support staff responsible for helping firms use the Agreement in practice.

item.title: When will there be justice for the sub-Postmasters? Fujitsu, CCRC, DBT and MoJ among witnesses in BTC’s first evidence hearing of the New Year
item.description: On Tuesday 6 January 2026 the Business and Trade Committee will convene a high-level evidence session on the continued search for justice and accountability in the Post Office Horizon scandal, described as the UK’s most widespread miscarriage of justice.

item.title: BTC: UK’s economic security regime “not fit for the future” – and risks becoming weak point in the West
item.description: Committee calls for Economic Security Bill to enshrine the approach set out in new Report in law, with the appointment of a dedicated Economic Security Minister.

item.title: What’s really behind the UK’s growth problem? Help the BTC help Government to fix it
item.description: Committee launches second annual roadshow on its priorities for growth with call for evidence

item.title: As Trump visits the UK for an unprecedented second State Visit, it’s time for “maximum pressure” to secure trade and technology alliance
item.description: In a report today the Commons Business and Trade Committee says President Trump’s State Visit is the moment to maximise pressure on the United States to agree final terms of a full Economic Prosperity Deal that will end sectoral uncertainty, secure critical supply chains and drive investment into the UK.

item.title: “No tweaks at the margins. Britain needs a new architecture of investment for a new age of risk” - BTC launches new inquiry on financing the real economy
item.description: In a new inquiry the Business and Trade Committee will examine the causes of the UK’s persistent underinvestment and explore how the UK can raise capital formation by mobilising greater levels of private and public finance—domestic and foreign—into British firms and infrastructure.

item.title: Committee welcomes the Horizon IT Inquiry Report, urges Government to “close painful chapter of history” and speed up justice for victims of Post Office Horizon scandal
item.description: The Business and Trade Committee welcomes the first report of the Post Office Horizon IT Inquiry published today. The recommendations of this report mirror much of what the Committee has called for – improvement of redress schemes, in particular the Horizon Shortfall Scheme, the need to speed up redress payments and the need for free, upfront legal advice to be available to all victims.

item.title: Britain Must Seize the Asia-Pacific Moment
item.description: Over the next five years, £7 trillion of new wealth will be created across Asia-Pacific— a quarter of global forecast growth and more than the entire GDP of the United Kingdom.
